vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current visual data. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) is absent/bare soil. P3 (Pothos) shows a single, struggling leaf. P4 (Silver Guest) is absent/bare soil.

plant_audit.P1: Pot is empty; soil appears undisturbed but devoid of plant matter.
plant_audit.P2: Pot is empty; soil appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P3: Single leaf remains, showing signs of chlorosis and turgor loss. The white rabbit anchor is present but the plant is failing.
plant_audit.P4: Pot is empty; soil appears undisturbed.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears consistently dark and damp across all pots, suggesting potential over-saturation or lack of drainage.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells scattered across the surface of the P3 pot, acting as a calcium supplement or soil amendment.
biome_observations.anomalies: The desk surface shows a white cable/wire intrusion near the yellow pot (P1). The absence of the registered specimens suggests a catastrophic failure of the indoor biome.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: From EARLIEST to CURRENT, there is a progressive disappearance of foliage. The P3 specimen has transitioned from a multi-leaf state to a single, drooping leaf. The other pots have transitioned from occupied to bare soil.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal decline. The lack of light (despite the LED) and likely over-watering have led to the death of the majority of the specimens. P3 is the only remaining biological entity, and it is in critical condition.
anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments in P3.
Total loss of P1, P2, and P4 specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a severe collapse of the botanical collection. The registry provided does not match the current physical reality, where three of the four pots are now devoid of life. The remaining P3 Pothos is severely compromised, likely due to environmental stressors such as insufficient light quality or root rot from over-saturation. The presence of eggshells indicates an attempt at intervention, which has failed to arrest the decline.
